title: Soil Metagenome PacBio V2

steps: Collect approximately 15 g of soil using a sterile soil corer or similar device and transfer to a sterile soil sieve and collection plate.
Once the solution has cleared, Quantify 1 µL of Sample using a Qubit 4 fluorometer or similar device.
Incubate the Sample at 37 °C for 00:10:00 then return the Sample to the magnetic rack.
Resuspend the beads in the water by flicking the tube until a homogenous solution has formed.
Remove the Sample from the magnetic rack and add 46 µL of molecular grade water to the beads.
Allow the beads to air dry for 00:00:30 or until the beads are satin in appearance. Do not over dry the beads, avoid excess EtOH carry over.
Promptly remove the excess EtOH with a P10 pipette and tip without disturbing the beads.
Remove the Sample from the magnetic rack and briefly centrifuge at < 1000 x g, °C, to collect any residual EtOH then return the Sample to the magnetic rack.
Repeat steps 43 and 44 then continue to step 46.
Aspirate and discard the supernatant without disturbing the beads.
Gently add 200 µL of 80 % (v/v) EtOH across the beads and incubate on the magnetic rack for at least 00:00:30 .
Once the Sample has cleared, aspirate and discard the supernatant without disturbing the beads.
Briefly spin down the Sample and place on a magnetic rack.
Incubate the Sample on a rotational mixer for 00:10:00 at Room temperature .
Add 60 µL of Room temperature Ampure XP beads to the Sample and mix by flicking the tube until a homogeneous solution has formed.
Allow the Sample to equilibrate to Room temperature and add 1 µL RNase A at 20 mg/mL . Mix tube by flicking and then incubate at Room temperature for 00:02:00 .
Centrifuge the Sample at 14000 x g, °C, 2 minutes . Discard the spin filter and retain the Sample eluate.
Incubate the Sample at 56 °C for 00:10:00 with intermittent agitation.
Add 100 µL of 56 °C DES elution buffer to the binding matrix and agitate the spin filter until a slurry has formed.
Allow the binding matrix to air dry for 00:02:00 .
Remove the spin filter from the catch tube and place it into a new and clean 1.5 mL lo-bind Eppendorf tube.
Centrifuge the Sample at 14000 x g, °C, 2 minutes to collect excess EtOH.
Repeat steps 29 and 30 then continue to step 32.
Centrifuge the Sample at 14000 x g, °C, 2 minutes then discard the flow through.

Add 500 µL of prepared SEWS-M buffer to the Sample bound binding matrix, close the lid securely and suspend the beads in the SEWS-M solution by flicking the tube. EtOH (100%) is a highly flammable liquid and vapour. It can cause serious eye irritation . Keep away from heat, hot surfaces, sparks, open flames and other sources of ignition.
Repeat steps 26 and 27 until all the binding matrix has been processed through the spin filter.
Centrifuge the spin filter at 14000 x g, °C, 2 minutes and discard flow through.
Remove the Sample from the rotational mixer and transfer 750 µL of sample to an MPbio spin filter using a wide bore P 1000 pipette tip. Return the Sample to the rotational mixer.
Add 1000 µL of DES to a new and clean 1.5 mL lo-bind tube and place the tube into an active heat block set to 56 °C .
Incubate the Sample on a rotational mixer at 30 rpm, Room temperature, 10 minutes .
Gently decant the Sample directly into the binding matrix without disturbing the protein pellet, secure the lid and mix the Sample by inversion until a homogeneous solution has formed.

Homogenise the binding matrix immediately before use. Add 1000 µL of resuspended binding matrix to a clean 5 mL lo-bind tube. Binding Matrix contains components that, when in contact with human tissue, may causeirritation. Wear personal protective equipment to prevent contact with the skin or mucousmembranes (gloves, lab coat, and eye protection).
Remove the Sample from the ice and centrifuge at 14000 x g, °C, 2 minutes to pellet the protein precipitate.
Incubate the Sample On ice for 00:10:00 to facilitate protein precipitation.
Decant the supernatant from the lysing matrix E tube into the pre chilled PPS without disturbing the pellet, then mix the Sample by inversion 10 times and place On ice .
Remove the lysing matrix-E tube from the ice and centrifuge at 14000 x g, °C, 4 minutes to pellet the debris.
Add 250 µL of protein precipitation solution (PPS) to a new and clean 1.5 mL lo-bind Eppendorf tube and chill On ice .
Repeat steps 14 and 15 then continue to step 17.
Incubate the Sample On ice for 00:05:00 .
Place the matrix tube in a MPBio FastPrep instrument (or similar) and homogenise for 00:00:10 at 5.0 ms then return the Sample to ice.
Add 122 µL of MT buffer to the Sample , mix by inversion and incubate On ice for 00:05:00 .
Resuspend the pellet in 978 µL of sodium phosphate buffer, then transfer the Sample to a new and clean lyzing matrix E tube.
Aspirate and discard the supernatant without disturbing the pellet.
Centrifuge at 5000 x g, °C, 4 minutes to pellet the Sample .
Transfer 1000 µL of the Sample to a new and clean 1.5 ml lo-bind Eppendorf tube using a P1000 pipette and wide bore pipette tip.
Defrost the Sample for 00:15:00 On ice prior to commencing DNA extraction.
Snap freeze and store Sample at -80 °C for future use.
Repeat step 5 until the total volume of sample has exhausted or the desired number of aliquots have been achieved.

Gently mix the bulk sample by hand to form a homogenous solution then aspirate 1000 µL of the total sample using a P1000 pipette and wide bore tip. Transfer the Sample into a new and sterile 2 mL cryovial and close the lid securely.
Incubate for at least 04:00:00 at room temperature or 4 °C Overnight with occasional mixing.

Use a top pan balance, spatular and weigh boat to weigh 10-50 g of homogenised soil Sample and transfer to a 250 mL conical flask. Promptly suspend the soil in 100 mL of Zymo DNA/RNA shield for a final concentration of 100-500 mg/mL .

Homogenise the soil sample by passing it through the soil sieve and collecting the output on the collection plate below the sieve. The use of a sterile plate sealer can be used to facilitate sieve homogenisation.
DNA blunting Prepare the following reaction on ice. Scale the reaction volume by sample number. A B 1 Reagent Volume (µl) 2 Enzyme Dilution Buffer 4.0 3 DNA Prep Additive 1.0

Prepare the following reaction in a 0.2 ml thin walled PCR tube for each sample and return to ice. Use DNA Prep Additive from step 53. A B 1 Reagent Volume (µl) 2 DNA 45.0 3 DNA Prep Buffer 7.0 4 NAD 1.0 5 DNA Prep Additive 1.0 6 DNA Prep Enzyme 1.0 7 Total 55
Mix the sample by flicking and spin down to collect.
Incubate the Sample in a thermal cycler at 37 °C for 00:15:00 then place the sample On ice .
DNA damage repair (FFPE). Add 2 µL of DNA damage repair mix v2 to each Sample and mix by flicking then spin down to collect.
Incubate at 37 °C for 00:30:00 then place the Sample On ice .
End-repair and A-tailing. Add 3 µL of End Prep Mix to each Sample mix by flicking and spin down to collect.
Incubate the Sample at 20 °C for 00:30:00 , then at 65 °C for 00:30:00 then place the sample On ice
SMRT bell adapter ligation Defrost one barcoded overhang SMRT bell adapter per sample, mix by flicking, spin down and place On ice
Add 5 µL of a single barcoded overhang SMRT bell adapter to each Sample , mix by flicking, spin down and place On ice .
Add 30 µL of Ligation mix, 1 µL of Ligation aditive and 1 µL of Ligation Enhancer to each sample, mix by flicking, spin down and return to ice.
Incubate the sample at 20 °C for 01:00:00 then at 4 °C for up to 16 h.
Incubate the Sample at 65 °C for 00:10:00 then place On ice .
Nuclease treatment Prepare the following reaction and place on ice. Scale the reaction volume by sample number. A B 1 Reagent Volume (µl) 2 Enzyme A 4.0 3 Enzyme B 1.0 4 Enzyme C 1.0 5 Enzyme D 2.0
Add 8 µL of Enzyme mix to eacg Sample and mix by flicking then spin down to collect.
Incubate at 37 °C for 01:00:00 then place the sample On ice .
Spri cleanup. Add 47.25 µL (0.45x) of SPRI beads at Room temperature and mix by flicking.
Incubate on a rotational mixer for 00:10:00 .
Briefly spin to collect the Sample .
Place the sample on a magnetic rack and allow the solution to fully clear.
Aspirate and discard the supernatant without disturbing the beads.
Add 200 µL of 80 % (v/v) EtOH across the beads and allow to sit for 00:00:30 .
Aspirate and discard the EtOH.
Repeat steps 74 and 75, then continue.
Spin down to collect the tube contents and return to the magentic rack.
Promptly remove residual EtOH with a pippette.
Add 31 µL of PB 1X Elution buffer to the beads and mix by flicking.
Incubate the Sample at 37 °C for 00:30:00 with occasional mixing.
Place sample on a magnetic rack and allow the sample to clear.
Aspirate 31 µL of Sample and transfer to a new 1.5 ml lo-bind tube.
Add 1 µL of Sample to 9 µL of water and mix by flicking.
Quantify 2 µL of diluted sample using a Qubit fluorometer and HS assay kit. Quantify 1 µL of diluted library on a tape station or similar fragment analyser.
Dilute SPRI bead clean up Add 350 µL of SPRI beads to 650 µL of 1 x PB elution buffer and mix by flicking.
Dilute the Sample to < 10 ng/µL with PB elution buffer.
Add 3.7 x volume of Sample of dilute SPRI beads to the Sample and mix by flicking.
Place on a rotational mixer for 00:30:00 at Room temperature
Briefly spin the sample to collect and place on a magnetic rack, then allow the sample to clear.
Aspirate and retain the supernatant in a new 1.5 lo-bind tube.
Add 250 µL of 80 % volume EtOH across the beads and allow to sit for 00:00:30 .
Remove and discard the EtOH without disturbing the beads.
Repeat the wash step and briefly spin to collect residual EtOH.
Remove residual EtOH then add 21 µL of 1x PB elution buffer to the beads and mix by flicking.
Incubate the Sample at 37 °C for 00:30:00 with occasional mixing then return to the magnetic rack.
Dilute 1 µL of final library in 9 µL of molecular grade water and retain for QC.
Aspirate 20 µL of final library to a new 1.5 ml lo-bind tube and place on ice.
Store final library at 4 °C for imminent SMRT cell loading or a -20 °C for longterm storage.
